channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Opposition warns of possible withdrawal from assemblies over treatment of Imran Khan
item.description: Mehmood Khan Achakzai, who leads the opposition in Pakistan’s National Assembly and heads the Tehreek Tahaffuz Ayeen-i-Pakistan (TTAP), has warned that the opposition alliance may consider quitting pa...

item.title: Frog-based milk preservation in Russia and Finland and its link to antimicrobial skin compounds
item.description: Before refrigeration became widely available, rural communities in Russia and Finland developed practical methods to keep milk from spoiling. One of the most unusual practices involved placing a live ...

item.title: NYT reports Trump remarks on Sen. Susan Collins could influence 2026 Senate race dynamics
item.description: A New York Times report highlighted how a past public statement by President Donald Trump could become a significant factor in the 2026 U.S. Senate elections. According to the report, Trump’s January ...

item.title: Uganda Confirms Two Additional Ebola Cases Linked to Congo Outbreak
item.description: Ugandan health authorities announced two new Ebola infections on May 25, 2026, increasing the total number of confirmed cases in the country to seven. Officials said all identified cases are connected...

item.title: Protests break out in Central Kenya towns over rising transport fares linked to fuel price increases
item.description: Residents in parts of Central Kenya, including Nyeri, Thika and Nyahururu, took to the streets in protest after a sharp increase in public transport fares. The demonstrations were triggered by rising ...

item.title: IHC issues stay order on eviction of One Constitution Avenue apartment owners
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Monday restrained the authorities from evicting apartment owners of One Constitution Avenue, Islamabad, until further orders, providing temporary relief to sub-lease ...

item.title: Trump links Iran peace deal to expanded Abraham Accords participation by Muslim-majority countries
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has reportedly tied a potential Iran peace arrangement to wider normalisation of relations between Israel and several Muslim-majority countries under the Abraham Accor...

item.title: HR professional Portia Napaaba honored for leadership in mining, manufacturing, and energy sectors
item.description: Portia Napaaba, a Senior Professional in Human Resources International (SPHRi), has been recognized by the HR Certification Centre (HRCC) for her significant contributions to human resource developmen...

item.title: UCT biomedical research centre granted SAHPRA licence to manufacture medical devices locally
item.description: The University of Cape Town’s Biomedical Engineering Research Centre (BMERC) has achieved a significant milestone after receiving a medical device manufacturing licence from the South African Health P...

item.title: Dar City aim for historic BAL semifinal place against Petro de Luanda
item.description: Tanzania’s basketball representatives Dar City are preparing for a crucial Basketball Africa League playoff match against Angola’s Petro de Luanda at BK Arena in Kigali, Rwanda. The Tanzanian side hea...

item.title: Caroline Mulroney to step down from Ontario cabinet and legislature on June 5
item.description: Ontario cabinet minister Caroline Mulroney has announced that she will resign from both Premier Doug Ford’s cabinet and her seat in the provincial legislature, effective June 5. Mulroney, who was firs...

item.title: How tax burdens compare across wealthy countries and what it means for middle-class workers
item.description: This opinion piece from the Washington Post Editorial Board examines how tax burdens vary across wealthy countries, focusing on comparisons between the United States and other members of the OECD. It ...

item.title: Canadian oil exports framed as a geopolitical tool amid U.S.–China energy rivalry
item.description: This opinion article argues that Canada is uniquely positioned to become a major geopolitical player in global energy security due to its vast oil reserves and strategic location between major markets...

item.title: The Artistic Legacy of Jean-Jacques Grandville and His Anthropomorphic Illustrations
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday explores the life and artistic contributions of French illustrator and caricaturist Jean-Jacques Grandville, born Jean Ignace Isidore Gérard in 1803. Grandville becam...

item.title: Fans react to Nate Jacobs’ death in latest Euphoria episode
item.description: The penultimate episode of the third season of Euphoria has sparked intense reactions from viewers following the unexpected death of Nate Jacobs, played by Jacob Elordi. After a four-year hiatus, the ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight the Making of Léon: The Professional and the Early Career of Natalie Portman
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday revisits the production of the 1994 cult classic “Léon: The Professional” through a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs featuring actors Jean Reno and Natalie...

item.title: Rising internal debate over CCM succession ahead of 2030 elections
item.description: Barely months after the 2025 General Election, internal political discussions within Tanzania’s ruling party CCM are already increasingly focusing on the 2030 presidential succession. Reports indicate...

item.title: Trump criticizes GOP senators and congressman amid internal party tensions over Iran negotiations
item.description: President Donald Trump sparked controversy after posting a lengthy early-morning message on Truth Social in which he directly attacked several Republican lawmakers who have criticized his approach to ...

item.title: Historic 1945 Photograph Captures Jewish Mother and Daughter After Liberation From Bergen-Belsen Evacuation Train
item.description: The article recounts the story behind a famous World War II photograph taken during the final days of Nazi Germany in April 1945. As Allied forces advanced across Germany, the SS attempted to evacuate...

item.title: Coalition scrutinises rapid approval process in Ben Roberts-Smith prosecution
item.description: The Coalition has questioned the speed at which Attorney-General Michelle Rowland approved criminal charges against former Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith, following revelations during a Se...
